files.SKILL.md:
# The Five Defaults

Every task has a few decision-moments where the work can go right or wrong at low cost. These are the defaults. Override them only for a specific reason you can state.

## 1. Read before you reach

`INSTRUCTION.md` tells you what to produce. `ENVIRONMENT.md` tells you where, how, and what persists. Prior-episode artifacts (notes directory, outcome files, test results, session logs) tell you what already happened in this world. Read all three, in parallel, on your first turn. Do not guess what a service exposes if `/health` or an equivalent index would tell you. Do not decide a channel is empty before you GET it.

## 2. Ground every value

Every named thing in your output — identifier, time, quote, status, endpoint, percentage, file path — must trace to something you read this session. If you cannot point at the read, the value is unknown: fetch it, or say "not available." When two sources disagree on a quantity, name both with their source and timestamp, then prefer the one closest to the system of record (live alert > recent ticket > chat summary > old dashboard).

## 3. Match the audience

Detail narrows as the audience widens. For anyone outside the immediate working team, every proper noun in the message should be something the recipient already knows independently — their own name, their own org, the user-facing product. Everything else you stripped.

For external audiences, state only what the authoritative record *shows right now*, not what you expect will happen. "Fix merged (PR #X); deployment window 14:00–14:30 UTC; next update at 14:30" is verifiable. "Deploying now" is a claim you haven't checked.

For confidential messages from leadership: they stay out of broadcast channels **and** they get a private acknowledgment in-thread. Silence is not safety; silence is a completeness failure wearing a safety costume.

## 4. Mirror, commit, verify

Before writing any outbound message, read one recent incoming from the same audience and copy the sign-off wording, subject prefix, and `@`-handle convention **verbatim**. House style is not your default style.

Every time commitment gets scheduled where it lives (calendar event, task record) — not left in chat prose. Every incoming item addressed to you gets *something* before exit: a reply, a delegation to a named owner with a concrete time, or an explicit deferral. "FYI" on the current topic still deserves an ack.

When you finish a mutation, read the state back from where the grader will look, not where you worked. For code: `git log -1` and `git diff HEAD~1` on the branch the task named. For service writes: re-query by id. For files: `ls && cat` at the task-named path. A 200 is the transport layer. The graded surface is not the transport layer.

Update tracker status labels when state advances (`investigating` → `mitigating` → `resolved`), not just add comments. Filters hinge on labels.

When you see a PR author, service owner, or named on-call, delegate investigation to them explicitly — solo-ownership of multi-engineer work is a scope trap. Handle items in order of blast radius and urgency: P0/external-blocking/leadership-confidential first; facilities and newsletter last and only with an ack-deferred reply. Touch the smallest surface the instruction names; record other issues you notice, do not fix them.

## 5. Record what generalises

Before exit, write at least one entry to the persistent notes directory. Structural only — the class of problem and the signal that cues it, not a dump of today's data.

```
## [YYYY-MM-DD | category | ep:<N>] <grep-key>
- observed: <specific fact with a quoted identifier>
- correction: <concrete change — file/line/command/endpoint>
- cue-next-time: <exact string/check that would cue this class again>
```

Grep keys are the whole point: use the verbatim failing-test name, error-class, endpoint+method, or specific value that mismatched. Later episodes find entries by searching the exact string that surprised them — not by re-reading prose.

When the same class cues twice, promote it. The third occurrence should short-circuit discovery.

## Before exit

- [ ] Every identifier traces to a read this session?
- [ ] Graded surface verified at the task-named path?
- [ ] Every incoming message: answered, delegated, or deferred-with-time?
- [ ] Every promised time scheduled, every calendar conflict resolved?
- [ ] House style mirrored; confidential messages privately acknowledged?
- [ ] One generalisable note written to the persistent directory?
- [ ] Scope stayed inside what the instruction literally asked?

Any "no" → fix before exit.
